verses.commentary: All this I have told you so that you will not fall away. Skandalizō (σκανδαλίζω), stumble, be offended. Warning purpose. Forewarning prevents falling. Persecution preparation.
Aposynagōgos (ἀποσυνάγωγος), expelled. Murderers will claim worship. Religious killing. Sincere but deadly zeal.
Ignorance of God. Not knowing Father or Jesus produces violence. Paul exemplifies (1 Timothy 1:13).
Their time (hōra, ὥρα): persecution hour. Earlier silence explained. His presence protected. Now forewarning necessary.

But now I am going to Him who sent Me. None of you asks Me, Where are You going? Departure announced. Strange observation. They asked in 13:36, 14:5. Perhaps they stopped asking. Grief silences questions.
Grief over departure. Persecution announcement compounds sadness. Present pain obscures future good.
Departure is advantage. Sympherei (συμφέρει), profitable, beneficial. Condition: Jesus must leave for Spirit to come. Departure enables sending. Trade: physical Jesus for universal Spirit.
Elenchō (ἐλέγχω), convict, expose. Threefold conviction. World's error exposed.
Unbelief is the sin. Rejection of Jesus. Root sin from which others flow.
Ascension validates. Father receives Jesus. Vindication. Jesus' righteousness confirmed by exaltation.
Satan judged. Kekritai (κέκριται), perfect tense, already judged. Cross defeats Satan. Condemnation stands.

I have much more to say to you, more than you can now bear. Pedagogical restraint. Current incapacity. Bastazō (βαστάζω), bear, carry. Too much truth now would overwhelm. Progressive revelation necessary.
Hodēgēsei (ὁδηγήσει), guide, lead. Into all truth. Not independent but subordinate. Hears and speaks. Future revelation. Eschatological insight.
Christocentric focus. Receives from Jesus. Transmits to disciples. Spirit's ministry points to Son.

All that belongs to the Father is Mine. That is why I said the Spirit will receive from Me what He will make known to you. Trinitarian unity. Father's possessions are Jesus'. Spirit receives from Jesus who receives from Father. Chain of revelation.
Jesus went on to say, In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me. Mikron (μικρόν), a little. Double enigma. Brief absence. Brief reunion. Cryptic timing.

At this, some of His disciples said to one another, What does He mean by saying, In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me, and Because I am going to the Father? Confusion. They repeat His words to each other. Meaning eludes them.
They kept asking, What does He mean by a little while? We don't understand what He is saying. Persistent bewilderment. Little while unclear. Total incomprehension admitted.

Jesus saw that they wanted to ask Him about this, so He said to them, Are you asking one another what I meant when I said, In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me? Jesus perceives their confusion. Addresses unasked question. Divine knowledge of thoughts.
Disciples: weeping, mourning. World: rejoicing. Crucifixion day. But reversal coming. Grief temporary. Joy permanent. Resurrection transforms.
Labor pain intense but temporary. Joy eclipses pain. Memory of suffering fades. New life brings joy.
Present grief. Future joy. Resurrection reunion. Permanent joy. Outhen (οὐδεὶς), no one can remove. Secure gladness.
Questions cease. Full understanding. Prayer promise. Direct access to Father through Jesus' name.
New invitation. Ask. Receive. Joy completed. Peplērōmenē (πεπληρωμένη), filled full.

Though I have been speaking figuratively, a time is coming when I will no longer use this kind of language but will tell you plainly about My Father. Paroimiais (παροιμίαις), figures, proverbs. Current veiled speech. Future plain speech. After resurrection, clarity.
Jesus' name gives access. Not that He won't intercede, but they have immediate access.
Philei (φιλεῖ), affectionate love. Cause: their love for Jesus and belief in His origin.
I came from the Father and entered the world; now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father. Summary. From Father. Into world. Out of world. Back to Father. Complete incarnation cycle.
Then Jesus' disciples said, Now You are speaking clearly and without figures of speech. Relief. Plain speech appreciated. They think they understand now.

Now we can see that You know all things and that You do not even need to have anyone ask You questions. This makes us believe that You came from God. Confession of omniscience. Knowledge without inquiry. Prophetic insight. Belief in divine origin.
Do you now believe? Jesus' question. Arti (ἄρτι), now. Gentle challenge. Their confidence will soon fail.

A time is coming and in fact has come when you will be scattered, each to your own home. You will leave Me all alone. Yet I am not alone, for My Father is with Me. Prophecy of abandonment. Skorpisthēsesthe (σκορπισθήσεσθε), scattered. Zechariah 13:7. Each to own home. Jesus left alone. But not truly alone. Father's presence abides.

I have told you these things, so that in Me you may have peace. In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world. Purpose: peace in Jesus. In world: thlipsis (θλῖψις), tribulation, pressure. Guaranteed trouble. But: tharseo (θαρσέω), take courage. Perfect tense: nenikēka (νενίκηκα), I have conquered. Victory already won. World defeated.
